macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Annual Inflation Surges to 2-Year High of 3.3% in March 2026
items.summary: US inflation hit 3.3% annually in March, the highest since May 2024, driven by a record 21.2% spike in gas prices due to geopolitical tensions and the war in Iran disrupting the Strait of Hormuz. Core inflation rose slightly to 2.6%, below forecasts, amid upcoming US-Iran talks.

items.title: US Q4 2025 GDP Growth Revised Down to 0.5% Amid High Energy Prices
items.summary: US GDP growth for Q4 2025 was revised to 0.5% from an initial 1.4% estimate, with consumer spending weakening due to elevated energy costs and sticky inflation above the Fed's 2% target. Global PMI data shows slowing output growth and surging input costs from energy.

items.title: Gas Prices Skyrocket 21.2% in March, Fueling Inflation Surge
items.summary: Gas prices jumped 21.2% in March, the largest monthly increase on record, pushing overall inflation to 3.3% amid the war in Iran and near standstill in Strait of Hormuz shipping. Only two ships passed through the strait on Friday.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $72K on US-Iran Ceasefire, Targets $80K Breakout
items.summary: Bitcoin hit $72,000+ following a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, triggering $427M in short liquidations, before consolidating above $71,000 amid predictions of an $80K breakout. The rally aligns with de-escalation in global tensions and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Crypto Futures See $103M Liquidations in 24 Hours Amid Volatility
items.summary: Crypto futures markets recorded approximately $103 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, reflecting heightened market activity and price swings.

items.title: Bitcoin Retreats to $71K After $73K Rejection, ETF Outflows Weigh
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $71,922 after failing to hold $73K, up 1.78% in 24 hours, while Ethereum rose 0.79% to $2,195 amid macro risks and $250M ETF outflows.

items.title: Ethereum Faces $54M Liquidations, Driven by Short Squeeze Post-Ceasefire
items.summary: Ethereum saw $54.4 million in liquidations over 24 hours, with $34.5 million from shorts, fueled by increased derivatives demand after the US-Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Trump-Backed WLFI Token Drops 12%+ on Liquidity and Debt Concerns
items.summary: World Liberty Financial's WLFI token fell over 12% after reports revealed it pledged 5B tokens to borrow $75M in stablecoins, draining a lending pool and sparking FUD; the project denied issues.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Rise on Cooler-Than-Expected CPI Data and Iran Peace Talks
items.summary: Bitcoin reached $73,111 and Ethereum $2,250 after US CPI inflation rose less than expected, boosting optimism despite war concerns; ceasefire with Iran added to gains.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Releases Gemma 4 Open-Source Model Family
items.summary: Google launched the Gemma 4 family of open-source AI models, runnable locally on laptops or phones without internet or subscriptions, outperforming many competitors like Claude and Llama in benchmarks with 26B and 31B parameter variants.[1][2]
items.source: YouTube - AI News Roundup & Google AI Studio

items.title: Anthropic Announces Claude Mythos Preview
items.summary: Anthropic unveiled a preview of Claude Mythos, described as a step-function change in agentic AI with advanced cybersecurity capabilities, alongside Claude Cowork product developments.[1][3]

items.title: Alibaba Releases Open-Source Wan 2.1 AI Video Model
items.summary: Alibaba open-sourced Wan 2.1, a new AI video generation model highlighted in recent AI news roundups.[1]

items.title: Nous Research Partners with Xiaomi for Free MIMO V2 Pro in Hermes Agent
items.summary: Nous Research partnered with Xiaomi (Shaomi) to offer MIMO V2 Pro—a 1T parameter model with 1M token context—free in Hermes Agent for two weeks, featuring self-improving agents and multi-platform support.[4]

items.title: Cursor 3.0 Major Update Released
items.summary: Cursor released version 3.0, a significant update to its AI coding tool, noted in the latest AI developments.[1]

items.title: OpenClaw Updates Challenge Anthropic's Claude
items.summary: OpenClaw received updates and reportedly outperformed Anthropic's Claude in comparisons, positioning it as a strong open alternative.[1][5]

items.title: Anthropic Acquires Coefficient Bio
items.summary: Anthropic acquired Coefficient Bio, expanding into bio-related AI applications.[1]

items.title: Bittensor Covenant AI Exits Key Subnets
items.summary: Covenant AI exited Bittensor subnets Templar (3), Basilica (39), and Grail (81), sparking community discussions on the decentralized AI network.[6]

items.title: Karpathy-Inspired Obsidian RAG for Claude Code
items.summary: Andrej Karpathy popularized an Obsidian-based RAG system integrated with Claude Code for long-term memory and self-updating knowledge management.[1][7]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
